claim: The cognitive revolution is compressed from 144 years (industrial revolution) to just a few years, creating unprecedented acceleration
actionable: Plan for rapid organizational adaptation; assume current workflows will be automated faster than historical precedent suggests

claim: $10 trillion US services market is only 0.2% automated by AI, representing massive growth opportunity
actionable: Identify services workflows you perform; 99.8% of the market has not yet been automated by AI, indicating high-value opportunities

claim: Work is shifting from 100% certainty outcomes to 1000x leverage with less certainty, requiring new mental models of quality
actionable: Train teams to accept higher-leverage, lower-certainty AI outputs; humans verify and correct instead of requiring perfect automation

claim: Academic AI benchmarks are dead; real-world measurement (like Expo competing in Hacker One) is the new gold standard for proving model capability
actionable: Design evals around real-world tasks your users face rather than relying on public benchmarks to validate model choices

claim: FLOPs per knowledge worker is the new production function; companies forecast 10x-10,000x increase in compute consumption per worker
actionable: Plan for massive compute scaling; budget assumes knowledge workers will use multiple agents in parallel, not single AI interactions

claim: Persistent memory (long-term context retention and stable agent identity) is unsolved and a major opportunity; no scaling laws exist yet
actionable: Invest in solutions for long-term agent memory and identity persistence; this is frontier work with high value

claim: Seamless communication protocols between AIs (beyond MCP) will unlock multi-agent shopping and coordination applications
actionable: Design agent workflows with clear handoff protocols; assume better inter-agent communication standards will emerge

claim: AI voice is ready now (high fidelity, low latency); AI video and real-time conversations are near-term opportunities
actionable: Build voice-first experiences for enterprise logistics and customer-facing automation; voice is viable now unlike video

claim: Open source AI is at a critical juncture; if it fails to compete, the AI future becomes limited to well-funded giants
actionable: Support and use open-source models to maintain a competitive, decentralized AI landscape
